name: Turkey Gumbo
recipeInstructions:
If you DON'T have any leftovers, begin here:
Place the turkey carcass in a large saucepot with water to cover. Sprinkle salt and pepper over the top. Stir and let boil for about 1½ hours. Remove the carcass and meat, reserving the broth. Proceed to the next step.
If you DO have leftovers, begin here:
Cut the leftover turkey into bite-sized pieces. Brown the flour in oil (use 3/4 cup) until it forms a dark roux. Add the chopped vegetables (onions, bell pepper, celery) and stir until they are soft. Stir in about a cup of hot broth, then add the roux to the stockpot with the reserved broth. Cook for half an hour, then add the tomatoes, turkey, seasonings, and ham. Continue cooking for another half hour. Serve over rice, file (ground sassafrass leaves), and Tabasco. Serves 8-10.
NOTE: Gumbo improves in flavor the longer it sits. It can be made ahead of time if needed. You may also add chopped okra (the term "gumbo" comes from an African word for okra) when you add your other vegetables, but be cautious as the okra will thicken the mixture quickly.
